{{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|ja|神 (神道)}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}} in the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|ja|神 (神道)}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}} in the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|ja|神 (神道)}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}} in the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|ja|神 (神道)}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}} in the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|ja|神 (神道)}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}} in the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|ja|神 (神道)}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}} in the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also! == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|ja|神 (神道)}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}} in the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[en:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami}}

Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=draft}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす|12=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[en:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami}}

Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|12=draft}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=draft}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|12=draft}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|ja|神 (神道)|12=draft}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=draft}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=draft}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=draft}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=draft}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=draft}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) : Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=draft}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=draft}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) : One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=draft}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) : Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす|12=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[en:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami}}

Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|タケミナカタ|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|ja|蓼科山|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ebisu|ja|えびす|12=draft|12=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[en:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami}}

revision.comment: 10 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:武居大伴主神 up to rev 98154621
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 武居大伴主神 | 画像 = 武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg | 画像サイズ = 250px | 画像説明 =
武居恵比寿社
（[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]）
| 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 武居大伴主神 | 別名 = 武居大友主神、武居会美酒、武居[[えびす|恵比寿]]、武居夷神、武居恵美志命、武居[[蝦夷]]命 | 別称 = 武居の長者 | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = | 配偶者 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = 武居恵比寿社 | 関連氏族 = [[今井氏（諏訪）|今井氏]] }} '''武居大伴主神'''（たけいおおともぬしのかみ、[[歴史的仮名遣]]：たけゐおほともぬしのかみ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 ==概要== 神名は'''武居大友主神'''とも表記される。また'''武居会美酒'''（たけい[[えびす]]・たけい[[蝦夷|えみし]]）、'''武居夷神'''とも呼ばれる。ただし『諏訪下社大祝武居祝系図』では「武居恵比寿神」は武居大友主神とは別神でその御子神とされる「[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 諏訪下社大祝武居祝系図]」『諏訪史料叢書 巻28』諏訪教育会、1938年、73頁。。 [[諏訪地方]]の先住神とされる。伝承によると、[[諏訪大社]]に祀られている'''[[建御名方神]]'''（諏訪明神）が諏訪に入ろうとした時、'''[[洩矢神]]'''と共に建御名方神と対抗するも敗れてしまい、2柱は建御名方神に仕える者となった「[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 守宅神と武居大伴主神と]」『信濃史蹟 上』福山寿久編、1910年、山田肇「健御名方命に降服した諏訪の國つ神 洩矢神及び武居大伴主惠美志命」『諏訪大明神』信濃郷土文化普及会〈信濃郷土叢書 第1編〉、1929年、74-88頁。[[宮地直一]]「[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1076393/39 諏訪地方の原始信仰]」『諏訪史 第2巻 前編』信濃教育会諏訪部会、1931年、62頁。宮坂光昭「古墳の変遷から見た古氏族の動向」『古諏訪の祭祀と氏族』 古部族研究会 編、人間社、2017年、79頁。。建御名方神が諏訪に踏み入る前に、進入者と妥協すべしと洩矢神を説得しようとすると洩矢神の怒りを買ってしまったという話もある今井野菊「大伴主」『神々の里 古代諏訪物語』国書刊行会、1976年、45-46頁。。 蓼科神社（[[北佐久郡]][[立科町]]）の社伝では、武居夷神（大伴主神か）が諏訪大神（建御名方神）に領土を譲ってから[[蓼科山]]に登ったと言われている{{Cite web|和書|title=蓼科神社|website=長野神社庁|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}}。 ==系譜== [[諏訪大社]]下社の神職の一つ・'''[[諏訪大社#神職|武居祝]]'''（たけいほうり）を務めてきた'''今井氏'''の始祖とされた[[太田亮]]『[https://books.google.co.jp/books?id=tv0VxgQEAscC&pg=PP175 信濃]』磯部甲陽堂、1924年、161頁。太田亮『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/17 諏訪神社誌 第1巻]』、官幣大社諏訪神社附属諏訪明神講社、1926年、15頁。。 ==神社== [[ファイル:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|サムネイル|
蛭子社（諏訪市）
]] *武居恵比須社（[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]武居） :諏訪大社下社秋宮の裏手にある。祭神は[[えびす|恵比寿神]]。 *蛭子社（武居会美酒）（[[諏訪市]]中洲神宮寺） :諏訪大社上社の[[諏訪大社#摂末社|下十三所]]の一つ。祭神は[[事代主神]]。付近には武居畑遺跡{{Cite web|和書|title=武居畑遺跡|website=なんでも諏訪百科|publisher=[[諏訪市博物館]]|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}}と武居城跡{{Cite web|和書|title=武居城|website=なんでも諏訪百科|publisher=諏訪市博物館|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0600/060706.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}}がある。 [[佐久市]]望月にある[[大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|大伴神社]]{{Cite web|和書|title=大伴神社|website=長野神社庁|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2048.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}}もその名称から武居大伴主神と関連付けされることもあった『北佐久郡志 第2巻』北佐久郡志編纂会編、国書刊行会、1983年、16頁。。伝承では一説に祭神を武居大伴主神とする{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== * [[タケミナカタ]] * [[諏訪大社]] * [[洩矢神]] * [[矢塚男命]] * [[蓼科山]] * [[えびす]] {{諏訪信仰}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけいおおともぬし}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神たけいおおともぬし]]

蛭子社（諏訪市）
]] *武居恵比須社（[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]武居） :諏訪大社下社秋宮の裏手にある。祭神は[[えびす|恵比寿神]]。 *蛭子社（武居会美酒）（[[諏訪市]]中洲神宮寺） :諏訪大社上社の[[諏訪大社#摂末社|下十三所]]の一つ。祭神は[[事代主神]]。付近には武居畑遺跡{{Cite web|和書|title=武居畑遺跡|website=なんでも諏訪百科|publisher=[[諏訪市博物館]]|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}}と武居城跡{{Cite web|和書|title=武居城|website=なんでも諏訪百科|publisher=諏訪市博物館|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0600/060706.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}}がある。 [[佐久市]]望月にある[[大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|大伴神社]]{{Cite web|和書|title=大伴神社|website=長野神社庁|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2048.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}}もその名称から武居大伴主神と関連付けされることもあった『北佐久郡志 第2巻』北佐久郡志編纂会編、国書刊行会、1983年、16頁。。伝承では一説に祭神を武居大伴主神とする{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== * [[タケミナカタ]] * [[諏訪大社]] * [[洩矢神]] * [[矢塚男命]] * [[蓼科山]] * [[えびす]] {{諏訪信仰}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけいおおともぬし}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神たけいおおともぬし]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|国津神|" to "|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|"
Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
Text replacement - "|建御名方神|" to "|建御名方神|1=Takeminakata|2=ja|3=タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata|"
Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|1=Takeminakata|2=ja|3=タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Kami|qq=}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|qq=}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|qq=}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|qq=}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|qq=}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[en:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|1=Takeminakata|2=ja|3=タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Kami|qq=}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|qq=}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|qq=}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|qq=}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|qq=}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]]

| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]]

Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q86728307]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q86728307]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =

Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|

]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q86728307]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[sn:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Takei-Otomo-no-Ookami]]

| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}}

([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}}
