However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}}

| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q86728307]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q86728307]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a [[:en:Kami|Kami]] associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]]. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tateshina, Nagano|Tateshina]], Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]]. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] * [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]] * [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q86728307]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Kami}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tateshina, Nagano|en|Tateshina, Nagano|WD=Q1346895|lt=Tateshina}}, Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|en|Mount Tateshina|WD=Q717453|lt=Mount Tateshina}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tateshina, Nagano|en|Tateshina, Nagano|WD=Q1346895|lt=Tateshina}}, Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|en|Mount Tateshina|WD=Q717453|lt=Mount Tateshina}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{Infobox deity}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Kami}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tateshina, Nagano|en|Tateshina, Nagano|WD=Q1346895|lt=Tateshina}}, Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|en|Mount Tateshina|WD=Q717453|lt=Mount Tateshina}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|1=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami|1=Moreya}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|en|Mount Tateshina|WD=Q717453|lt=Mount Tateshina|1=Mount Tateshina}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[ja:武居大伴主神]] [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{Infobox deity}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami (武居大伴主神) | image = [[File:武居恵比須社 - Takei-Ebisu Shrine 03.jpg|250px]] | caption =
Takei Ebisu Shrine
({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, [[Suwa District]], [[Shimosuwa]])
| kami_type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami | other_names = Takei Ōtomo no Kami, Takei Ebisu, Takei Emishi, Takei Ebisu no Mikoto, Takei Ebisunomikoto | titles = Takei no Chōja | shrines = {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | related_clans = {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏（諏訪）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Takei Ōtomonushi no Kami''' (武居大伴主神, historical kana: たけゐおほともぬしのかみ) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Kami|1=Kami}} associated with local folklore and the {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}} in the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. == Overview == The deity's name is also written as '''Takei Ōtomo no Kami''', '''Takei Ebisu''', or '''Takei Emishi'''. Additionally, the 'Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram' identifies "Takei Ebisu no Kami" as a distinct deity, considered the child of Takei Ōtomo no Kami.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185913/45 |title=Suwa Shimosha Daihōri Takei Kei Diagram |publisher=Suwa Historical Collection |date=1938}} Takei Ōtomonushi is regarded as one of the original deities of the Suwa region. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} (Suwa Myōjin), enshrined at {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}, encountered resistance from Takei Ōtomonushi and {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami|1=Moreya}}. However, both were defeated and subsequently became vassals of Takeminakata.{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/765072/29 |title=Guardian Gods and Takei Ōtomonushi |publisher=Shinano History Collection |date=1910}} Other versions suggest disputes between Moreya and Takeminakata over entering Suwa.Yamada Hajime, 'Takei Ōtomonushi as a Vassal,' Suwa Daimyōjin, 1929. According to the traditions of {{ill|Tateshina Shrine|ja|蓼科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateshina Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tateshina, Nagano|en|Tateshina, Nagano|WD=Q1346895|lt=Tateshina|1=Tateshina, Nagano}}, Takei Ōtomonushi surrendered his territory to Takeminakata and ascended Mount Tateshina.{{Cite web|title=Tateshina Shrine|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2121.htm|access-date=2019-07-25}} == Genealogy == Takei Ōtomonushi is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja|武居祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Horibe (Suwa Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} priestly lineage, which was historically managed by the {{ill|Imai Clan (Suwa)|ja|今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Clan (Suwa)|qq=}}.Ōta Ryō, ‘Shinano,’ Isobe Kōyōdō, 1924. == Shrines == [[File:蛭子社（武居会美酒） - Takei-Ebisu Shrine.jpg|thumb|
Ebisu Shrine (Takei Ebisu)
]] * {{ill|Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja|武居恵比須社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) **Located behind the {{ill|Autumn Shrine|ja|秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Suwa Taisha Shimosha. Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}}. * {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|蛭子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takei Ebisu) (Suwa City) **One of the subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Kamisha. Includes the ruins of Takei Hata and Takei Castle.{{Cite web|title=Takei Hata Ruins|url=https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/0100/010212.htm}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} (Saku City, Mochizuki) **Traditionally linked to Takei Ōtomonushi, though evidence is speculative.‘Saku County Gazetteer,’ 1983. == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|1=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami|1=Moreya}} * {{ill|Mount Tateshina|en|Mount Tateshina|WD=Q717453|lt=Mount Tateshina|1=Mount Tateshina}} * {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|武居大伴主神|version=98154621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武居大伴主神]] {{wikidata link|Q86728307}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
